demo.modeActiveDescription: Data and actions are simulated. To use your account and backend, exit demo mode.

ads.trailerEmbedUnsupported: Embedded playback is not available on this device (e.g. TV).

requests.myRequestsExplanation: This page lists all the media you have requested (films, series). Each request goes through an approval process: pending, approved or declined. When approved, the media is automatically downloaded.
requests.noRequests: You haven't requested any content yet.
requests.requestAlreadyExists: A request already exists for this media
requests.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to cancel this request?

requestsAdmin.title: Manage requests
requestsAdmin.description: Approve or decline user media requests. Approved requests trigger automatic downloads.
requestsAdmin.explanation: When users request films or series (via the "Request" button on a media page), their requests appear here. As an administrator, you can approve or decline each request. Approved requests trigger an automatic download of the selected media.

feedback.requireCloud: Sign in with a cloud account to send feedback.
feedback.newMessageDesc: Send a message to the team to report an issue or suggestion.
feedback.newReplyBody: You have received a new reply to your feedback

blacklist.title: Blacklist
blacklist.description: Exclude films and series from discovery and requests
blacklist.explanation: The blacklist allows you to exclude certain films or series from discovery and from being requested. Blacklisted media will not appear in the home page carousels and users cannot request them.

discover.slidersExplanation: Sliders are the themed carousels displayed on the home page (e.g. "Trending films", "Popular series"). They let you customize what content is shown to users. Initialize the default sliders to get started, then enable or disable them as needed.
discover.noTorrentsYet: No torrents have been found for this media yet. Use the Request button to ask an administrator to add it. Once approved, the download will start automatically.
discover.requestAlreadySubmitted: You have already requested this media.
discover.description: Customize home page carousels (trending films, popular series, etc.)
discover.pageSubtitle: Discover films and series to request

syncCategories.title: Sync categories
syncCategories.description: Choose which torrent categories (films, series, others) should be synchronized for each indexer.
syncCategories.selectorDescription: Enable or disable torrent categories for this indexer. If you select nothing, all categories will be synchronized by default.

language.title: Choose your language
language.description: Select the interface language. You can change it at any time in the settings.

serverUrl.title: Server Configuration
serverUrl.description: Enter the URL of your Popcornn backend server
serverUrl.step1FirstTimeSubtitle: Connect this client to your server: scan the QR code from your phone (or another already-configured device), or enter the server URL here.
serverUrl.step1ReconfigureSubtitle: Change the server URL or add this device via the QR code.
serverUrl.choiceQuestion: How would you like to proceed?
serverUrl.choiceFirstTime: This is my first time setting up
serverUrl.choiceFirstTimeDesc: I've never configured Popcornn. I want to enter the server URL here or do the setup from my phone.
serverUrl.choiceAlreadyConfiguredDesc: Another device (phone, TV, computer) is already connected. I want to scan its QR code or enter the code to get the configuration.
serverUrl.choiceLocalAccountDesc: I received an invitation (local account). The server URL is already provided or I'll enter it — no need to scan a QR code or enter a code on popcorn-web.
serverUrl.backToQrOrCode: Back to QR code or code
serverUrl.mobileChoiceIntro: Choose how to connect this device:
serverUrl.mobileJoinExisting: I already have an account and server
serverUrl.mobileJoinExistingDesc: Scan the QR code shown on an already-configured TV or computer to get the server URL and sign in.
serverUrl.mobileFirstInstallDesc: Enter your server URL manually, then continue setup on this device.
serverUrl.scanQRCodeDesc: Allow camera access to scan the QR code shown on your TV or computer
serverUrl.enterCodeDesc: If you can't scan, enter the 6-character code shown on the other device
serverUrl.manualConfigDesc: Enter the server URL manually (first-time setup)
serverUrl.cameraPermissionHelp: To scan the QR code, you must allow camera access in the app settings.
serverUrl.qrScanErrorHelp: Make sure the QR code is visible and the camera works. You can also enter the code manually below.
serverUrl.tvStep1: On your phone, open the Popcornn app or popcorn-web (signed in to your account).
serverUrl.tvStep2: Scan this QR code or open the link shown.
serverUrl.tvStep3: On the page that opens: sign in if needed, set the server URL if not done yet, then tap "Authorize".
serverUrl.tvStep4: Go to popcorn-web and enter this code to authorize this device.
serverUrl.tvQrAlsoSetupFromPhone: You can do the full initial setup from your phone: open the link (QR or code), sign in to your account, set the server URL if needed, then authorize this code.
serverUrl.tvWaitingAuth: Waiting for authorization from your phone...
serverUrl.tvOrEnterCode: Code to enter on popcorn-web (same as in the QR code)
serverUrl.openPopcornWebToAuthorize: Open popcorn-web to authorize (code included in link)

errors.invalidUrlFormat: Invalid URL format. Use: http://192.168.1.100:3000 or https://your-domain.com
errors.invalidProtocol: Protocol must be http:// or https://
errors.connectionFailedDetails:
Check that:
• Popcornn server is running
• The IP address is correct
• Your device and server are on the same Wi-Fi network
errors.backendNotAccessibleDetails: Check that the Popcornn server is running and the URL is correct
errors.quickConnectInitErrorDetails: Check your internet connection and try again
errors.codeExpiredDetails: Connection codes expire after a few minutes. Generate a new code.
errors.codeInvalidDetails: Make sure you scanned the correct QR code or entered the correct 6-character code
errors.connectionErrorDetails: An error occurred. Check your internet connection and try again
errors.noBackendUrlInCloudDetails: Your cloud account has no server URL. Use manual configuration to enter your server URL.
errors.authorizationErrorDetails: An error occurred during authorization. Try again or use manual configuration
errors.qrCodeInvalidDetails: The scanned QR code is not valid. Make sure you scan the code shown on your TV or computer.
errors.networkErrorDetails: Cannot reach the server. Check your internet connection and that the server is accessible.

serverUrl.sameOriginConfirmMessage:
The API server (backend) address is the same as this page's address (client). Often, the client and API are on the same domain via a reverse proxy (e.g. nginx).

Confirm that you want to use this address for the backend?

disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.description: Please read and accept the following terms before continuing
disclaimer.acceptTerms: I accept the terms of use
disclaimer.content: This application is intended for personal use only. The user is responsible for ensuring that their use complies with the laws in force in their country.

auth.title: Sign in / Sign up
auth.description: Sign in with your Popcornn.app account

welcome.title: Welcome!
welcome.cloudBackup: Back up my configuration to the cloud
welcome.cloudBackupDescription: Your settings will be automatically synced and restored on all your devices
welcome.importConfigDescription: Restore your configuration from the cloud
welcome.importExplanation: Everything saved in your cloud account is imported: indexers, TMDB key, download location, sync settings, language. Only items present in the cloud are listed below.
welcome.importedConfigTitle: Configuration imported — you can edit the items below:
welcome.importOnlyIndexersHint: Only indexers were present in the cloud. To also import the TMDB key, download location and sync settings, check “Sync configuration to the cloud” at the end of the wizard on an already-configured device.

indexers.title: Indexers Configuration
indexers.description: Add your indexers to search for content

tmdb.title: TMDB Configuration
tmdb.description: Configure your TMDB API key to enrich metadata

downloadLocation.title: Download Location
downloadLocation.description: Choose where to save your downloaded files
downloadLocation.stepIntro: Enter the folder where the backend saves torrents. This path depends on how you run the server:
downloadLocation.contextDev: Development (local backend): path relative to the backend working directory (e.g. downloads or ./downloads) or an absolute path (e.g. C:\Users\...\Downloads).
downloadLocation.contextDocker: Docker: the path is inside the container (e.g. /app/downloads). Configure the volume in docker-compose (e.g. ./data/downloads:/app/downloads) to access files on the host.
downloadLocation.contextWindowsDaemon: Windows daemon (service): path on the machine where the backend runs (e.g. C:\Popcorn\downloads). Set DOWNLOAD_DIR or the service config so the backend uses this folder.
downloadLocation.pathSavedHint: Path saved. The backend will use this folder for downloads.
downloadLocation.realPathLabel: Real path used by the backend

complete.title: Setup Complete!
complete.description: Your application is ready to use

account.title: My Account
account.interfaceSettingsDescription: Customize language, theme and display preferences in the Interface section of settings.
account.logoutConfirm: Are you sure you want to sign out?

twoFactor.title: Two-Factor Authentication
twoFactor.enterCode: Enter the code received by email

quickConnect.title: Quick Connect
quickConnect.description: Scan the QR code with your phone (popcorn-web), sign in then authorize this device. You can also enter the code manually.
quickConnect.scanHint: Scan to open popcorn-web: sign in then authorize this device
quickConnect.codeExpired: Code expired. Click to generate a new code.

account.recoverConfigFromCloudDescription: Restore saved configuration (indexers, TMDB key, download location, sync settings) from your cloud account.
account.recoverConfigFromCloudNoConfig: No configuration saved in the cloud.

devices.title: Connected devices
devices.description: List of devices that have connected to your Popcornn cloud account. This list is shared across all your clients (desktop, web, TV, etc.).
devices.empty: No devices registered for this cloud account.
devices.footerInfo: In a future version you’ll be able to revoke devices you don’t recognize from here.

interfaceSettings.themeDescription: Choose the interface appearance (light, dark or automatic based on system)
interfaceSettings.autoplayDescription: Automatically start playing the next episodes
interfaceSettings.skipIntroDescription: Show a button to skip the opening credits during series
interfaceSettings.skipIntroAutoDescription: When starting an episode, automatically skip the opening credits
interfaceSettings.nextEpisodeButtonDescription: Show a button to skip to the next episode near the end
interfaceSettings.introSkipSecondsDescription: Intro duration in seconds (button and auto-skip)
interfaceSettings.nextEpisodeCountdownDescription: Number of seconds before end to show the button
interfaceSettings.streamingModeDescription: Choose the playback system: HLS (recommended), Lucie (WebM) or direct stream (alternative mode).
interfaceSettings.streamingModeHlsDescription: Adaptive streaming with .ts segments (compatible with all media)
interfaceSettings.streamingModeLucieDescription: 5-second WebM segments with VP9+Opus (modern, requires browser support)
interfaceSettings.streamingModeDirectDescription: Direct playback without transcoding (limited to natively supported formats)
interfaceSettings.streamingDownloadFullCardDescription: Download and play media immediately, without waiting for the full download to complete. Subscribers only.
interfaceSettings.streamingDownloadFullDescription: Allows you to start playing media as soon as the download begins, without waiting for it to fully download. Choose between downloading the entire file in the background or only the part being played. Subscribers only with the streaming option.
interfaceSettings.streamingDownloadFullRequiresSubscriptio: Streaming mode (and this option) require an active subscription with the streaming torrent option.
interfaceSettings.streamingRetentionDescription: How long to keep torrents downloaded while streaming. After this delay, they are automatically deleted to free up space.
interfaceSettings.librarySectionDescription: Customize language, quality and pagination for library lists
interfaceSettings.showZeroSeedTorrentsDescription: Include torrents with 0 seeders on Films and Series pages (some may be unavailable)
interfaceSettings.torrentsInitialLimitDescription: Number of films/series shown on first load (20-500)
interfaceSettings.torrentsLoadMoreLimitDescription: Number of items added on each scroll (20-200)
interfaceSettings.torrentsRecentLimitDescription: Number of items in the recent additions section (20-200)

qualitySettings.title: Media Quality
qualitySettings.subtitle: Manage the video player playback quality and the preferred quality for downloaded media
qualitySettings.cardDescription: Playback quality and preferred quality for films and series
qualitySettings.playerQualityDescription: Default resolution when starting a video
qualitySettings.playerQualityAutoDesc: The player automatically selects the best quality based on your connection
qualitySettings.playerQualityHighestDesc: Always forces the highest available resolution, regardless of connection
qualitySettings.playerQualityLowestDesc: Forces the lowest resolution to save bandwidth
qualitySettings.mediaQualityDescription: Priority quality for films and series shown on the dashboard, Films and Series pages
qualitySettings.mediaQualityNote: The chosen quality is prioritized in results. Other qualities remain visible and accessible.
qualitySettings.mediaQualityAllDesc: No preference, shows all media without distinction
qualitySettings.mediaQuality1080pDesc: Full high definition, optimal quality for most screens
qualitySettings.mediaQuality4kDesc: Ultra high definition, large files, ideal for big screens

playback.streamingFallbackToHls: Switched to HLS playback due to direct stream issue.
playback.errorStream: Unable to play video. Check server connection.
playback.torrentUnavailableOnIndexer: This torrent is no longer available on the indexer or could not be retrieved. Choose another source.
playback.streamNotReadyYet: Stream is not ready yet (torrent still initializing). Wait 1–2 minutes and try again.
playback.maxTranscodingsReached: Maximum number of transcodings reached. Stop other ongoing playback.
playback.maxTranscodingsReachedWithList: Maximum number of transcodings reached (max {max}). Stop other playback or wait for it to finish. Active transcodings: {list}
playback.transcodingsEvicted: Other transcodings have been stopped to free up capacity.

settings.title: Settings
settings.uiPreferencesDescription: Customize the interface: theme, language, display

uploadTrackerPanel.title: Publishing
uploadTrackerPanel.description: Create a .torrent for a library media and publish it via P-upload to your trackers.
uploadTrackerPanel.noMedia: No media in library. Add library sources and run a scan first.
uploadTrackerPanel.mediaAlreadyPublished: Already published to all selected trackers (hash not needed).
uploadTrackerPanel.mediaExistsOnTracker: A similar version seems to already exist on the tracker (via indexer).
uploadTrackerPanel.c411PrereqsBody: After creating the .torrent, add it to your BitTorrent client (qBittorrent, etc.) and make sure you are seeding: the Status column should show "Seeding" or "Partage", and the Seeds column at least 0 (1). C411 will reject the torrent if you are not seeding at submission time. For reuploads, keep the original file name and have the .nfo file.
uploadTrackerPanel.c411NamingBody: Word separator: the dot (no spaces or dashes). No accents or non-standard characters. Keep the original team tag on reupload; no personal tag without original creation. If source tag is missing: use -NOTAG suffix. Full rules by category (films, series, audio, etc.) on the C411 guide.
uploadTrackerPanel.c411DescriptionNfoBody: Each upload must have a complete description (synopsis, image, technical info) and a readable .nfo file that exactly reflects the content. The NFO is the technical sheet of the release. Useful tools: C411 assistant, FervexPrez, DUPEFR, nfo123, MediaInfo. Details by category on the C411 guide.
uploadTrackerPanel.nfoContentLabel: NFO file content (optional, for C411 upload)
uploadTrackerPanel.nfoContentPlaceholder: Paste the content of your .nfo file here, or leave empty
uploadTrackerPanel.c411NotConfiguredHint: C411 indexer is not configured. Enter cookies or passkey below to enable this card.
uploadTrackerPanel.cookieFormatHint: Colle ici tes cookies (une ligne « Nom=valeur » ou en-tête Cookie: …). On extrait __Host-c411_session et __csrf.
uploadTrackerPanel.orSeparator: — ou remplis les deux champs ci-dessous —
uploadTrackerPanel.passkeySaved: Passkey saved (announce URL will be used from server).
uploadTrackerPanel.fieldsClearedHint: The fields below are cleared after each save for security. Your configuration is saved on the server. The announce URL is filled automatically when a passkey is saved. To change cookies or passkey, enter new values and save again.
uploadTrackerPanel.announceUrlFilledByPasskey: If you saved your passkey, this URL is filled automatically when the page loads.
uploadTrackerPanel.trackerUrlHint: To add this tracker to an existing torrent: in your client (rqbit), open the torrent → Trackers → add this URL. For a C411 upload, use the .torrent file provided by C411.
uploadTrackerPanel.cookiesOrPasskeyRequired: Enter at least: cookies (session + CSRF) or a passkey.
uploadTrackerPanel.passkeyHint: Used to build https://c411.org/announce/YOUR_PASSKEY. Saved so you don't have to paste the full URL each time.
uploadTrackerPanel.uploadAssistantDescription: Native upload engine to publish torrents to configured trackers.
uploadTrackerPanel.uploadAssistantEnabledLabel: P-upload is enabled for tracker uploads.
uploadTrackerPanel.uploadAssistantPathHint: This page uses the native P-upload engine. No external script or specific path is required.
uploadTrackerPanel.createTorrentAndPublish: Create .torrent and publish to selected trackers
uploadTrackerPanel.publishToC411Label: Automatically publish to C411 after creating the .torrent
uploadTrackerPanel.creatingTorrentProgress: Creating .torrent (hashing file pieces — may take several minutes for large files), then sending to selected trackers…
uploadTrackerPanel.creatingTorrentProgressStepsHint: Steps: 1) Create .torrent (file hashing) 2) Send to trackers
uploadTrackerPanel.successCreate: Torrent created and saved in the server's uploads folder.
uploadTrackerPanel.successCreateAndC411: Torrent saved in backend uploads folder and sent to C411: {url}
uploadTrackerPanel.successCreateAndTracker: Torrent saved in backend uploads folder and sent to {tracker}: {url}
uploadTrackerPanel.notSentToC411: Not sent to C411 (save cookies in settings for automatic upload).
uploadTrackerPanel.fieldsRequired: Please select a media, at least one tracker, and enter the announce URL or a passkey.
uploadTrackerPanel.cookiesRequired: Session and CSRF cookies are required for publishing.
uploadTrackerPanel.publishAllDescription: Publish all library media to C411 in one go. Uses saved cookies.
uploadTrackerPanel.processAllDescription: For each media: create the .torrent (if not already created) then publish via P-upload to the selected trackers. Already-processed media are skipped as we go when possible.
uploadTrackerPanel.trackersDescription: Select one or more trackers. The native P-upload engine will run one upload per tracker.
uploadTrackerPanel.wizardChooseMediaDescription: Select the library media to create a .torrent and publish to your selected trackers.
uploadTrackerPanel.wizardChooseMediaListDescription: Select one or more media entries to upload to your selected trackers.
uploadTrackerPanel.wizardChooseTrackersDescription: Select one or more trackers. Configure required fields for each selected tracker.
uploadTrackerPanel.wizardReviewDescription: Check the summary below, then launch the upload. Each tracker will be processed in sequence.
uploadTrackerPanel.cancelRequestedSuccess: Cancel requested. The creation will stop shortly.
uploadTrackerPanel.externalCreationInProgress: A .torrent creation is already running for this media ({progress}%). Wait for that job to finish before launching again.
uploadTrackerPanel.alreadyOnTrackerHint: If a tracker returns that a media is already present (duplicate), it is removed from the list for next uploads.
uploadTrackerPanel.mediaValidationDescription: Validation before screenshots: checks file accessibility, size > 0, ffprobe read (duration + video stream), and a small ffmpeg decode probe.
uploadTrackerPanel.mediaValidationGlobalError: One or more media files are invalid/corrupted. They were excluded from selection.
uploadTrackerPanel.mediaInvalidUploadBlocked: Media invalid/corrupted, upload is blocked until the file is repaired or replaced.
uploadTrackerPanel.confirmDeleteMediaFile: Delete this media from disk and library? This action is irreversible.
uploadTrackerPanel.mediaFileRemoved: Media file deleted and library entry removed.
uploadTrackerPanel.wizardC411ApiKeyHint: Required for P-upload (API). Get it from your C411 profile.
uploadTrackerPanel.wizardSummaryDistributionHint: {media} media will be processed across {trackers} tracker(s).
uploadTrackerPanel.wizardTorrentFactoryHint: Live progression for every pipeline step (configuration, preparation, preview and upload).
uploadTrackerPanel.screenshotsHint: Generated automatically when you publish (requires FFmpeg).
uploadTrackerPanel.screenshotsStepDescription: Generate screenshots for the release (requires FFmpeg). They will be included in the tracker description when you publish.
uploadTrackerPanel.publishModalNote: NFO and description are ready. Screenshots will be generated when you publish if FFmpeg is available.
uploadTrackerPanel.trackersWipHint: Only C411 is fully implemented for now. Other trackers are being added progressively and may return a “not implemented” message.
uploadTrackerPanel.noConfiguredTrackers: No configured tracker is currently available for the upload assistant.
uploadTrackerPanel.manageTrackersDescription: Manage trackers used by the assistant (default activation, hide/restore and C411 variables).
uploadTrackerPanel.manageC411Hint: C411 settings used by the upload assistant.
uploadTrackerPanel.previewDescriptionSameAsNfo: The description sent to the tracker (C411) is the same as the NFO.
uploadTrackerPanel.previewPublicationTrackerHint: Preview of the description sent to C411 (sections, technical info, Popcornn.app footer).

clientTorrentsList.title: Add tracker
clientTorrentsList.description: Add a tracker URL to a client torrent.
clientTorrentsList.addTrackerTitle: Add the tracker URL above to this torrent
clientTorrentsList.addSuccess: Tracker added. It will be used on next resume/start.
clientTorrentsList.addTrackerErrorHint: If the error persists, ensure the torrent client (librqbit) is running on the server and that its API supports adding trackers.

seedingDiagnostic.title: Seeding diagnostic
seedingDiagnostic.description: Check port, UPnP and ratio mode to identify sharing issues.
seedingDiagnostic.subtitle: Torrent client, UPnP and tracker compatibility mode status. Use the links below to test tracker announce and port.
seedingDiagnostic.upnpHowToEnable: To enable: set environment variable LIBRQBIT_UPNP=1 or config [torrent] enable_upnp_port_forwarding = true. Server restart required.
seedingDiagnostic.portHint: The TCP and UDP port to open on your router is not yet available. Restart the server, then come back to this page.
seedingDiagnostic.portHintWithPort: Open port {port} (TCP and UDP) on your router to allow incoming connections (seeding / ratio). If you have double NAT (modem + router), configure port forwarding on both devices. Test it with the tool below.

reseedPanel.title: Re-seed
reseedPanel.subtitle: Download .torrent files from the backend to re-add them to your torrent client.
reseedPanel.description: Download .torrent files to resume seeding in your client.
reseedPanel.noTorrents: No torrents recorded in the backend.
reseedPanel.downloadTitle: Download the .torrent file for this torrent
reseedPanel.noFileHint: Torrents without a .torrent in the database were added via magnet or without indexer sync. Get the .torrent from the torrent page on the tracker (e.g. C411) and add it to the client pointing to the existing folder.
reseedPanel.libraryDescription: Media already on your disk (library). If a .torrent is in the database, you can download it and add it to your torrent client with the same path to resume seeding without re-downloading.
reseedPanel.librarySummary: {total} library item(s): {withFile} with .torrent in DB, {withoutFile} without.
reseedPanel.libraryNoFileHint: For items without a .torrent in the database, the file may come from a local source or a magnet-added torrent. You can try to automatically restore the .torrent from an indexer.
reseedPanel.searchIndexerDescription: Choose an indexer and let Popcornn try to find and re-attach .torrent files to library media to put them back in seed.
reseedPanel.prepareReseedTitle: Rename file on disk to match the .torrent so the client can find the data
reseedPanel.dbChangedHint: After changing the torrent client database, use Refresh to see the updated list. The restore message below can be cleared with the button below.

myUploadsPanel.title: My uploads
myUploadsPanel.description: List both successful and failed uploads, check rqbit presence, view tracker targets, and download .torrent files.

errors.insufficientStorage: Insufficient disk space. Free up space or upgrade your plan.
errors.forbiddenMessage: You don't have the required permissions to access this page.
errors.notFoundMessage: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.
errors.serverErrorMessage: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again later.

permissions.accessDeniedDescription: This feature is reserved for the main account. Local users have limited access to settings.

subscriptionGuard.title: Paid subscription required
subscriptionGuard.description: This feature is reserved for paying subscribers. Subscribe to an active plan to access it.

dashboard.noContent: No content is available at the moment.
dashboard.downloadUnavailable: Unable to start the download for this content.
dashboard.recentAdditionsEmpty: No new releases at the moment.

search.title: Search
search.placeholder: Search for a movie or series...
search.startSearchDescription: Search for movies or series using the search bar above
search.indexerSearchNote: No local results. Popcornn is querying indexers.
search.mustBeLoggedIn: You must be logged in to search for content
search.noTorrentsUseRequest: No torrents found for this search. You can request these media to have them added.

library.title: Library
library.emptyDescription: Downloaded media will appear here automatically.
library.scanStarted: Scan started successfully. The library will be updated automatically.
library.seriesGroupedHint: Series are grouped: one card = one series (multiple episodes).

mediaDetail.packAddTorrentHint: After adding the torrent, episodes will be listed here by file (S01E01, …).

downloads.title: Downloads
downloads.torrentsWillAppear: Torrents you add will appear here
downloads.confirmRemove: Do you want to remove this torrent{withFiles}?
downloads.confirmRemoveAll: Do you want to remove all torrents?
downloads.logsFiltered: {count} log(s) displayed (repetitions filtered) • Auto-refresh every 5 seconds
downloads.verificationTimeout: Verification pending (timeout). Download may still be in progress.
downloads.nopeersWarning: No peers found. This torrent may not be available right now.
downloads.confirmDeleteTorrentMessage: Remove this torrent from the client and delete the files from disk? This action cannot be undone.
downloads.addTrackerUrl: Add or change a tracker URL
downloads.trackerAdded: Tracker added. It will be used on next resume.
downloads.notLinkedToTmdb: Not linked to a media (manual add or not synced yet)

settingsMenu.title: Settings
settingsMenu.subtitle: Configure your application according to your preferences

overviewCard.sameOriginBackendTitle: Client and API server on same address
overviewCard.sameOriginBackendDescription: This page's address (client) and the API server address (backend) are the same. To use a different server, change the backend URL.
overviewCard.ratioStatsSubtitle: Total downloaded, total uploaded (client) and tracker ratios when available.

forceCleanup.title: Cache cleanup
forceCleanup.description: Removes inactive HLS/transcoding cache directories (videos not currently playing). Frees space and stops orphaned ffmpeg processes.

transcodingConfig.title: Transcodings
transcodingConfig.description: Limits how many HLS/FFmpeg streams can run at once. Lower values reduce CPU load. On NAS (e.g. ZimaCube), setting 1 is recommended to avoid high resource usage.

restartBackend.title: Restart backend
restartBackend.description: Restarts the Popcornn server. In Docker, the container will restart automatically. In dev, your runner/watcher will relaunch the backend.
restartBackend.confirm: Restart the backend now? Requests will be interrupted for a few seconds.
restartBackend.success: Restart requested. The backend should be back in a few seconds.

resources.title: Resources
resources.description: Server memory and CPU, and whether hardware acceleration (GPU) is available for FFmpeg.
resources.gpuNotAvailableHint: In Docker: expose the GPU in the stack. On CasaOS, use the « Popcornn (VAAPI GPU) » (Intel/AMD) or « Popcornn (Nvidia GPU) » variant so acceleration is available.
resources.unavailableOrOldBackend: Unable to load resources. If your server has not been updated recently, this feature may be unavailable (endpoint /api/media/resources).

resourcesMonitorDev.title: Resource monitor
resourcesMonitorDev.description: Real-time server CPU and memory usage.
resourcesMonitorDev.tipSyncInterval: Increase sync interval (Settings > Sync) to reduce idle load.
resourcesMonitorDev.tipLibraryScan: Reduce library scan frequency or set interval to 0 if not needed.
resourcesMonitorDev.tipStartupScan: Library scan at startup is disabled by default; keep POPCORN_LIBRARY_SCAN_ON_STARTUP unset.

setup.title: Setup
setup.description: Reopen the setup wizard to configure your client

uiPreferences.title: Preferences
uiPreferences.description: Customize the interface: theme, language, display

server.title: Server
server.description: Configure the Popcornn server URL to connect to

tmdb.title: TMDB
tmdb.description: TMDB API key for metadata enrichment

indexers.title: Indexers
indexers.description: Configure your TMDB API key (required) and your indexers to search and download torrents

flaresolverrPanel.intro: FlareSolverr lets you access indexers whose site shows a Cloudflare challenge (« Checking your browser »).
flaresolverrPanel.configureBackend: The FlareSolverr URL is set on the server (FLARESOLVERR_URL environment variable, e.g. in Docker/CasaOS).
flaresolverrPanel.perIndexer: For each affected « custom » indexer, check « Use FlareSolverr » in the indexer add/edit form.
flaresolverrPanel.statusConfigured: FlareSolverr is configured on this server.
flaresolverrPanel.statusNotConfigured: FlareSolverr is not configured on this server. Set FLARESOLVERR_URL (e.g. http://flaresolverr:9191) to enable it.
flaresolverrPanel.testUnreachable: FlareSolverr is not responding or unreachable.

syncCategories.title: Sync categories
syncCategories.selectorDescription: Select the categories you want to sync for this indexer

indexerDefinitions.title: Indexer definitions
indexerDefinitions.subtitle: Manage shared indexer definitions (country, language). Create, edit and delete according to your permissions.

sync.title: Torrent sync
sync.description: Manage automatic torrent synchronization from indexers

diagnostics.title: Diagnostics
diagnostics.description: Test backend connectivity, version and indexers

storage.title: Storage
storage.description: Disk space used and plan limit

librqbit.title: Torrent client
librqbit.description: Configure and monitor the integrated torrent client

librqbitWeb.title: librqbit Web
librqbitWeb.description: Open the torrent client web interface in a new tab

mediaPaths.title: Media folders
mediaPaths.description: Set download folders by type (movies, series)

mediaPathsPanel.intro: Choose the folder where downloads are saved for each type. Paths are relative to the server root.

librarySourcesPanel.intro: Add folders containing movies or series (e.g. on another NAS). After validation, a scan and TMDB enrichment will run.

librarySourcesPanel.pathFormatWindows: Windows (Explorer): open the folder in Explorer, then copy the path from the address bar → \\computer\share\folder

librarySourcesPanel.pathFormatNote: The path must be accessible from the machine running the server (on Linux, use the mount point, e.g. /mnt/nas/Movies).
librarySourcesPanel.addSuccess: Folder added. Scan running in background.
librarySourcesPanel.noSources: No external folders. Add a path (movies or series) to include it in the library.

serverSettings.title: Server Configuration
serverSettings.storageInfo: This URL is stored in localStorage and used by the Astro client API routes to proxy to the Rust backend. The Rust backend uses port 3000 by default.
serverSettings.urlStoredInfo: This URL is stored in localStorage and used by the client API routes.
serverSettings.invalidUrl: Invalid URL. Expected format: http://ip:port or https://domain.com
serverSettings.protocolError: Protocol must be http:// or https://
serverSettings.connectionSuccess: Connection successful! The Rust backend is accessible.
serverSettings.connectionError: Unable to connect to Rust backend ({status}). Check that the URL is correct and that the Rust backend is running.
serverSettings.savedMixedContent: URL saved. Test skipped because the page is HTTPS and the backend is HTTP (Mixed Content).
serverSettings.mixedContentError: The page is HTTPS and the backend is HTTP. The browser blocks this test (Mixed Content). Use an HTTPS backend or a reverse proxy.
serverSettings.sameOriginConfirmMessage:
The API server (backend) address is the same as this page's address (client). Often, the client and API are on the same domain via a reverse proxy (e.g. nginx).

Confirm that you want to use this address for the backend?

info.title: Information
info.point1: The Rust backend URL is stored in localStorage (client-side)
info.point2: This URL is used by the Astro client API routes to proxy to the Rust backend
info.point3: Make sure the Rust backend is running and accessible at this URL
info.point4: The Rust backend uses port 3000 by default (configurable via BACKEND_PORT)

cookieWizard.title: Get session cookie
cookieWizard.simpleIntro: Use a browser extension to copy the cookie (including HttpOnly). Cookie-Editor is free and open source.
cookieWizard.simpleStep1: Install the Cookie-Editor extension: Chrome or Firefox (see links below).
cookieWizard.simpleStep2: Open the tracker site in a tab and log in (complete reCAPTCHA if shown).
cookieWizard.simpleStep3: Click the Cookie-Editor icon in the browser bar. Find the session cookie (often named after the site or "session"), click the copy icon next to its value.
cookieWizard.simpleStep4: Return to Popcornn and paste (Ctrl+V) into the "Cookie (session)" field, or use the drop zone below.
cookieWizard.extensionNote: Other extensions (e.g. EditThisCookie) work too. The key is being able to copy the session cookie value from the logged-in tracker page.
cookieWizard.manualIntro: If you prefer to get the cookie manually (Developer Tools), follow the steps below. Screenshots can be added later.
cookieWizard.manualStep1Text: Open the tracker URL (same as the indexer base URL) in your browser. Log in with your account if needed (and complete reCAPTCHA if it appears).
cookieWizard.manualStep2Text: Press F12 (or right-click → Inspect). Go to the Application tab (Chrome/Edge) or Storage (Firefox), then Cookies and select the tracker site.
cookieWizard.manualStep3Text: Find the session cookie (often named after the site or "session"). Right-click the value → Copy, or double-click to select and copy (Ctrl+C). You can also copy the full Cookie line if your browser shows it.
cookieWizard.manualStep4Text: Return to Popcornn, in the indexer form, and paste (Ctrl+V) into the "Cookie (session)" field. Save. The cookie expires after a while; if search fails later, repeat these steps and update the field.

form.name: Name
form.cookiePlaceholder: Paste the session cookie value here
form.cookieHelp: This indexer uses cookie-based login. To get it: (1) Open the indexer site in your browser and log in (complete reCAPTCHA if shown). (2) Open Developer Tools (F12) → Application (or Storage) tab → Cookies → select the site. (3) Find the session cookie (often the site name or "session") and copy its full value. (4) Paste it here. The cookie expires after a while; if search starts failing, log in again on the site and update this field.
form.cookieWizardOpen: Guide: how to get the cookie
form.useFlareSolverrHelp: Recommended if the site shows a Cloudflare challenge. Backend must have FLARESOLVERR_URL configured.
form.manualTrackerRatioHelp: Optional · used when the ratio cannot be fetched automatically from the tracker.

indexerDefinitionDocs.title: Documentation — indexer definition
indexerDefinitionDocs.intro:
This documentation explains how to fill every field of an indexer definition.

Tip: if unsure, start from an existing indexer and adjust only the endpoint + mappings.

fields.id:
Stable unique identifier. Used as indexerTypeId on the client. Recommended: lowercase, digits, hyphens.
Example: "ygg-torznab"
fields.name:
Display name shown to users.
Example: "YggTorrent"
fields.version:
Definition version (useful to track changes).
Example: "1.0.0"
fields.description:
Optional description (shown in the list).
Example: "FR indexer via Torznab"
fields.protocol: Protocol/type. "torznab" is the most common (Jackett/Prowlarr). Use "rest" / "newznab" / "custom" depending on your API.
fields.searchEndpoint:
Path (or absolute URL) called to perform a search.
You can use a "{indexer}" placeholder if required by your endpoint.
Example: "/api/v2.0/indexers/{indexer}/results/torznab"
fields.searchMethod: HTTP method used by the endpoint (GET or POST).
fields.searchParams:
JSON of parameters to send. For Torznab: often empty here because the query is built by the indexer.
You can set static params if your endpoint needs them.
fields.responseMapping:
JSON mapping from API response fields to expected fields (title, id/guid, size, seeders, leechers, link, uploaded_at...).
Values represent the key name in the response.
fields.categoryMapping:
JSON mapping from category -> indexer category code(s).
Example: films=2000, series=5000 (Torznab).
fields.ui: Optional UI metadata (icon, color, custom fields). Used to display the indexer nicely in the client.
fields.requiresApiKey: If checked: the UI should treat the API key as required when configuring the indexer.
fields.requiresAuth: If checked: the indexer requires authentication (in addition to / instead of an API key).

examples.torznabIntro: Typical setup (Torznab via Jackett/Prowlarr). Users will then provide baseUrl + API key when configuring the indexer.
examples.torznabSnippet:
protocol: torznab
searchEndpoint: /api/v2.0/indexers/{indexer}/results/torznab
searchMethod: GET
country: FR
language: fr
categoryMapping: { "films": "2000", "series": "5000" }
responseMapping: { "results": "Results", "title": "Title", "id": "Guid", "size": "Size", "seeders": "Seeders", "leechers": "Peers", "uploaded_at": "PublishDate", "link": "Link" }
examples.note: Note: depending on your backend/proxy, baseUrl is provided by the user when configuring the indexer (not in the definition).

mistakes.endpoint: Endpoint: make sure it starts with "/" (if relative) and matches the target API.
mistakes.json: JSON: watch commas, quotes and braces. Invalid JSON will make create/update fail.
mistakes.mapping: Mapping: if a field is incorrectly mapped (e.g. id/title), results may be empty or incomplete.
mistakes.countryLanguage: Country/Language: fill them to allow filtering and avoid ambiguous definitions.

ratioAdmin.title: Tracker mode
ratioAdmin.subtitle: Manage tracker compatibility mode and view statistics
ratioAdmin.modeDescription: When enabled, the client adapts tracker announces during download and then continues in normal mode once the torrent is complete (seeding).
ratioAdmin.statsDescription: Total upload/download and indicators computed from client-managed torrents.
ratioAdmin.testDescription: Check connection to torrent client and mode status.
ratioAdmin.testSeedDescription: Sends an announce to the tracker like the torrent client (uploaded/downloaded/left). Set the amount in MB to report.
ratioAdmin.testSeedHowToKnow: If the announce succeeded, a green box appears with "Tracker response: 200 OK". On failure, a red error message appears at the top.
ratioAdmin.addTrackerButtonTitle: Add this tracker URL to the torrent (used on next resume/start)

ratioAdmin.testSeedTrackersNone: No trackers found for this torrent. Enter the URL below.
ratioAdmin.testSeedTrackersApiMissingKey: The torrent client API does not return trackers (use a client built from this repo). Enter the tracker URL below.
